ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general muffin class; no specific blueberry crumble muffin subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Muffins and similar sweet baked goods
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated blueberry crumble muffin entry; A000E (sweet baked goods / muffins) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code for blueberry crumble muffin; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard blueberry crumble muffin weighing approximately 110 grams, as commonly served at cafes and bakeries in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Muffin, blueberry, commercially prepared (FDC ID 168880)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared blueberry muffin used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are broadly consistent with USDA reference ranges for this food category. Input values retained and cross-validated against USDA FDC ID 168880. Minor differences attributable to crumble topping addition increasing fat and sugar slightly. Nutrient values accepted as plausible and within expected range for this food type.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 59 derived from University of Sydney GI Database entry for blueberry muffin (commercially prepared). This is classified as medium GI (56–69). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (59 × 50.0g × 1.10) / 100 = 32.45 per 110g serving. The crumble topping adds marginal additional sugar and fat but does not materially alter the GI category relative to a standard blueberry muffin.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (27.3g/100g) combined with moderate sodium (318mg/100g) and low dietary fibre (2.3g/100g) creates an unfavourable cardiovascular profile per AHA 2018 dietary guidelines.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit blueberry crumble muffins; a single 110g muffin delivers approximately 30g of sugar — exceeding the AHA daily added sugar limit for women — and 350mg sodium, both of which contribute to elevated blood pressure and endothelial stress.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 27.3g/100g far exceeds the 20g/100g glycaemic ceiling; carbohydrate at 50g/100g with only 2.3g/100g dietary fibre produces a rapid and significant postprandial glucose spike; medium GI of 59 combined with high glycaemic load of 32 per serving makes this food unsuitable for blood glucose management.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid blueberry crumble muffins entirely; the combination of 27g sugar, 50g refined carbohydrate, and minimal fibre per 100g produces a high glycaemic load per serving that will cause a sharp postprandial glucose excursion inconsistent with glycaemic control targets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Trans fat at 0.45g/100g, saturated fat at 3.18g/100g, cholesterol at 45.45mg/100g, and high added sugar collectively elevate LDL cholesterol, promote endothelial inflammation, and increase cardiovascular risk per AHA/ACC 2019 guidelines.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid blueberry crumble muffins; the presence of trans fat (0.45g/100g), saturated fat (3.18g/100g), and 27g/100g added sugar in a single serving contributes meaningfully to atherogenic lipid profiles and systemic inflammation, both of which are primary drivers of adverse cardiovascular outcomes.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 27.3g/100g far exceeds the 15g/100g RA refined sugar threshold, strongly promoting pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; refined wheat flour and butter further contribute to the inflammatory burden.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid blueberry crumble muffins, particularly during flares; the high sugar, refined flour, and saturated fat content collectively promote systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Blueberry Crumble Muffin is a Western-style sweet baked good consisting of a soft, moist muffin base enriched with fresh or frozen blueberries and topped with a crunchy streusel or crumble layer made from butter, flour, and sugar. The muffin base is prepared from wheat flour, sugar, eggs, butter or oil, milk, and baking powder, producing a tender, slightly dense crumb with a golden exterior. The crumble topping adds textural contrast and additional sweetness. Blueberries contribute a mild tartness, natural anthocyanin antioxidants, and a small amount of vitamin C (1.82mg/100g). Per 100g, this muffin provides 363.64 kcal, 5.0g protein, 16.36g fat (including 3.18g saturated fat and 0.45g trans fat), 50.0g carbohydrate, 27.27g sugar, 2.27g dietary fibre, and 318.18mg sodium. The glycaemic index is approximately 59 (medium GI), but the glycaemic load per standard 110g serving is approximately 32 — classified as high — making this food clinically problematic for blood glucose management. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sugar, moderate sodium, trans fat, and low fibre profile. Commonly sold at cafes, bakeries, and food delivery platforms in Singapore as a breakfast or snack item. Classified as vegetarian; contains gluten, eggs, dairy, and wheat allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a blueberry muffin?
Is blueberry muffin good for weight loss?
Can I eat blueberry muffin if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared blueberry muffin (FDC ID 168880). Values are broadly consistent with the USDA reference range for this food category. Confidence elevated from input 0.7 to medium due to successful cross-validation; not rated high because the exact product (with crumble topping) was not found as a discrete entry in an approved primary source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific blueberry crumble muffin formulation is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values were not reported in the input record and are not available for this specific product formulation in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. Two portions were listed in the input with conflicting is_default flags (120g and 110g both marked is_default=true); 110g adopted as the standard serving size as it aligns more closely with the USDA reference serving for a commercially prepared muffin. The input listed 'country_of_origin: Singapore' reflecting the point of sale; the food itself is of Western (US/UK) culinary origin. Halal status not confirmed; marked is_halal=false pending certification verification. GI of 59 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for blueberry muffin (commercially prepared); the crumble topping variant was not separately indexed.
